description: This figure presents the mechanical properties of Fe-27.5Mn-9.1Al-0.92C alloy as a function of aging temperature after 16 hours of heat treatment. The graph contains four distinct curves: ultimate tensile strength (σb), yield strength (σ0.2), elongation (δ), and reduction of area (ψ). The x-axis represents aging temperature ranging approximately from 450°C to 600°C, while the left y-axis indicates strength values in kg/mm² and the right y-axis shows ductility indicators in percentage. As aging temperature increases, both σb and σ0.2 exhibit upward trends, with σb reaching a peak value of approximately 95 kg/mm² at 550°C before declining, whereas σ0.2 reaches around 85 kg/mm² at 550°C. Conversely, the ductility parameters δ and ψ decrease significantly with increasing temperature, reaching minimum values at 550°C (δ approximately 7-8%, ψ approximately 10%) followed by a slight recovery at higher temperatures. These trends demonstrate that 550°C represents the optimal aging temperature for achieving maximum strength, albeit with minimum ductility. Above 550°C, strength decreases and ductility increases due to the coalescence of the dispersed precipitate phase, consistent with the precipitation hardening mechanism described in the accompanying text.
